topic: A student learns their thesis advisor is leaving the university
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the stark finality of a closing door. My fingers hovered over the screen, the cursor blinking mockingly on the subject line: "Departure Notification." I reread the text three times, a slow, heavy rhythm taking over my breathing. Professor Albright, the architect of my entire dissertation, was leaving. The sheer weight of the news settled in my chest, a sudden, cold pressure. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric groaning under my weight, and stared blankly at the ceiling tiles. My mind felt momentarily hollowed out, unable to process the sudden vacuum where direction used to reside.

[story 2]

I was walking to the library when I saw the notice posted on the departmental bulletin board. My pace faltered. The paper felt suddenly flimsy in my hand. Albright. Gone. I stopped dead in the hallway, the ambient noise of students shuffling and distant chatter receding into a dull hum. A strange lightness washed over me, quickly followed by a disconcerting sinking sensation in my stomach. I gripped my backpack straps so tightly my knuckles turned white. *This can't be happening,* I thought, the realization hitting me with a sharp, unwelcome jolt. I needed a moment, just a breath, before I could even consider what to do next.

[story 3]

When I finally opened the departmental announcement, the words swam before my eyes. My hand trembled as I traced the lines of the message. It was official. Professor Albright was stepping down. A silence descended around me, thick and absolute, muffling the usual sounds of the academic building. I stood frozen by my desk, the half-finished manuscript lying abandoned on the blotter. I blinked rapidly, trying to force the information to settle, but it kept slipping away, leaving a strange, dizzying sensation in my head. I slowly pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal on linoleum sounding deafeningly loud in the quiet room.
